factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: News explicitly states 'Gold prices rise as cooling rate hike bets hit dollar' (2026-07-05T23:21). Reduced Fed hiking expectations lower real yields, which is the single strongest structural support for gold.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 161.70 and DXY implied stronger on safe-haven bid; BoJ yield curve steepening (10y JGB at 2.79%, 30-year high) supports carry unwind and dollar strength. Stronger USD typically inversely correlates with gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk / Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: Iran tensions (Strait of Hormuz shipping concerns, Ali Khamenei funeral, Russia-Ukraine war) support safe-haven positioning, but tanker flows are normalizing and oil prices are falling, reducing the acute crisis premium. Fragile ceasefire in Red Sea.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying (Structural)
factors.summary: Hong Kong pension fund easing gold ETF rules for HK$1.53 trillion pool (2026-07-06T02:42) signals meaningful structural bid. Goldman revised central-bank demand to ~60 tonnes/month through 2026, firming the bullish case despite year-end target cut of $500 in June.

factors.name: Oil & Inflation Dynamics
factors.summary: Oil prices falling (OPEC+ flagging higher supplies, Hormuz flows normalizing, 'oil glut' narrative). Lower oil = lower near-term inflation, which reduces urgent Fed tightening pressure but also reduces gold's hedge value in inflationary scenarios.

factors.name: US Equity Futures / Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 ES futures +0.4%, NQ +1.2% at open (2026-07-05T22:03). Risk-on mood and AI momentum (Samsung expected 18-fold profit jump) typically pressure gold as investors rotate into equities; gold competes as alternative allocation.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent)
factors.summary: Australia TD-MI Inflation Gauge came in at -0.4% m/m (vs -0.3% prior, 2026-07-06T01:46), signalling disinflation. No major US CPI/PCE in news window, but cooling inflation narrative supports lower-for-longer rate expectations.

factors.name: BoJ & Yen Carry Trade Risk
factors.summary: BoJ JGB yields at 30-year high (2.79%) while JPY weakens to 40-year lows; intervention risk remains live. If BoJ hikes or markets reprrice yen higher, carry unwind could spike risk-off and initially benefit gold, but strong dollar pressures it.

factors.name: Wall Street Gold Forecasts (Dispersion)
factors.summary: Wide range of 2026 targets: BofA $4,800 (cautious), Goldman revised lower by $500, JPMorgan $6,000 (bullish). Dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty on structural vs. cyclical drivers; no consensus directional conviction.

factors.name: RBNZ Rate Decision (Upcoming Wed)
factors.summary: NZ central bank rate decision Wednesday; market split on hold vs. hike (2026-07-06T03:06, 2026-07-06T00:30). Unclear catalyst for gold, depends on language and global rate repricing signals.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war between structural tailwinds (cooling Fed hike expectations, strong central bank buying, geopolitical risk premium) and headwinds (USD strength from higher real rates and safe-haven positioning, equity rally on AI momentum, normalizing oil/inflation). The 4h and 24h declines are modest (-0.28% and -0.49%), suggesting consolidation rather than a trend. Recent news emphasizes 'cooling rate hike bets' as supportive for gold, yet simultaneous USD/JPY strength and equities climbing on chip earnings push in the opposite direction. Wall Street's wide forecast dispersion ($4,800–$6,000) underscores lack of clear consensus.
catalysts_ahead: RBNZ rate decision (Wednesday 2026-07-09)
US CPI/PCE release (date TBD, likely early-to-mid July)
ECB signals (Moulin/Nagel commentary suggests tightening cycle may be complete)
Hormuz strait shipping normalization updates (risk premium fade if trend continues)
Samsung, SK Hynix earnings (Tuesday 2026-07-08) — if miss expectations, risk-off could support gold
Fed minutes / Powell speech (if scheduled, would clarify rate path)
screening.risk_warning: Risk to NEUTRAL bias: (1) If US equity rally extends on strong chip earnings, risk-on sentiment could push gold lower despite dovish rate narrative. (2) If BoJ surprises with hawkish guidance or intervention, rapid yen strength could spark carry unwind and initial gold spike, but strong USD would follow. (3) If Hormuz tensions reignite suddenly, geopolitical premium would re-inflate gold fast. (4) If inflation data surprises hot before FOMC, real yields will spike and gold will sell off sharply. (5) Upcoming RBNZ decision could trigger cross-asset repricing if unexpectedly hawkish.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is too mixed (competing drivers) and move is not yet extended to warrant aggressive entry. Wait for next major catalyst (CPI or RBNZ) to reveal direction; on confirmation of dovish inflation/Fed surprise, consider long above $4,200 resistance with stop below $4,150. If risk-on continues (equities break higher), watch for exhaustion before considering shorts.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Market repricing suggests dovish tilt post-NFP (July hike odds at 24%, Sept at 55%), which would lower real yields and be BULLISH for gold, but repricing is still in early stages.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Latest Messaging
factors.summary: Friday's softer NFP (June non-farm payrolls missed) triggered dovish repricing: July hike probability collapsed from ~50% to 24%. However, no fresh FOMC decision or Powell speak today; next major event is Fed Minutes this week (date TBD, likely Wed/Thu).

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding firm near 101.00 and gaining ground in early Asian trade per FXStreet (04:35 UTC). USD strength post-soft jobs data suggests carry trades and real yield differentials remain attractive, pressuring gold via inverse DXY correlation.

factors.name: USD/JPY & Yen Intervention Risk
factors.summary: USD/JPY rallied to 162.20+ early Monday, testing towards 163.00 (40-year highs) per FXStreet. Goldman revised yen forecast to 165/USD (most bearish on Street). Yen intervention risks remain, but current momentum is USD-bullish, which is headwind for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Data
factors.summary: No CPI/PCE in this window; next major inflation print is US CPI due Tuesday next week (July 8). Current consensus leans toward continued moderation post-soft NFP, which is mildly bullish gold (lower real yields), but not confirmed.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Energy
factors.summary: Brent near $71.90, WTI near $68.50–68.70, both trading under pressure. OPEC+ expanded production target by 188k bpd; analysts expect further downside. Oil glut fears rising. Lower oil = lower inflation risk = less Fed support = headwind for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions
factors.summary: Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei funeral underway; China tested nuclear-capable missile in South Pacific; Russia struck Kyiv killing 11 ahead of NATO summit; Strait of Hormuz shipping risks cited. Safe-haven bid should support gold, but USD is capturing most of it.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: Hong Kong pension fund easing gold ETF investment rules (HK$1.53 trillion exposure), marking structural demand tailwind. No fresh PBoC/RBI announcements, but structural bid remains supportive.

factors.name: Equities & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: US stock futures up on Sunday session extending last week's dovish repricing rally (S&P 500 strongest week since early May). Risk-on sentiment is mildly bearish for safe-haven gold, but equities not in euphoria (no VIX data given).

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold +0.32% in 4h, +0.03% in 24h—near flat. Extended pullback from two-week high ~$4,200 stalled above $4,150. Resilience hints at support, but no fresh conviction move either direction.

screening.narrative: Gold extended last week's modest pullback after softer-than-expected NFP triggered dovish repricing (July Fed hike odds collapsed to 24%). However, USD strength has reasserted early Monday—DXY near 101, USD/JPY testing 162+, and carry trades remain attractive—limiting downside for gold. Geopolitical tensions (Iran, Ukraine, China missile test, Hormuz risks) offer some safe-haven bid, but are being overwhelmed by dollar strength. Oil weakness (OPEC+ output expansion, glut fears) signals lower inflation risk, which is mildly bullish gold long-term but not immediately supportive. Next major catalyst is US CPI on Tuesday, which will dictate whether the dovish repricing holds or reverses.
catalysts_ahead: US ISM Services PMI (Monday, likely this session)
Fed Minutes release (Wed or Thu this week, likely thurs 7/11)
US CPI print (Tuesday, July 8 – HIGH IMPACT)
RBNZ monetary policy decision (Wednesday, July 8)
ADP employment change (Tuesday, July 8)
Japan average cash earnings (Tuesday, July 8)
Geopolitical escalation risk (Hormuz, Iran, Ukraine, NATO summit dynamics)
screening.risk_warning: A stronger-than-expected CPI print next Tuesday could reverse the dovish repricing, shock real yields higher, and send USD/DXY surging—which would be sharply bearish for gold. Conversely, if CPI disappoints and signals entrenched disinflation, real yields could drop further and gold could break through $4,200 with conviction. Yen intervention (BoJ/MOF jawboning or direct action) could also shock USD/JPY lower, depressing carry demand and triggering gold strength. Current consolidation near $4,150–$4,200 is vulnerable to both directions until CPI confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 consolidation after pullback from $4,200 high. No strong fresh conviction. Recommend waiting for either: (1) break above $4,200 with 4h close + volume confirmation (bullish), or (2) CPI surprise next Tue to reset regime. Short-term: avoid chasing; monitor ISM Services PMI today for marginal clues.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: USD firming at week start with DXY retaking 101.00 and USD/JPY testing new 40-year highs near 162.30. Stronger dollar directly headwind for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Policy
factors.summary: Post-NFP, July Fed hike odds collapsed to 24% (from higher prior) and September at 55%; dovish repricing supports gold. However, no fresh FOMC statement or Powell speech in news window to shift baseline.

factors.summary: WTI down 0.64% to ~$68.30; Saudi cuts main crude grade by most in 26 years; OPEC+ oversupply fears emerging. Lower oil eases inflation risk, reducing gold's inflation-hedge appeal and supporting USD via deflation narrative.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Iran, Ukraine, Middle East)
factors.summary: Khamenei funeral procession ongoing; Strait of Hormuz traffic restored after tanker U-turns; Ukraine air attacks continue. Mixed signals—no acute escalation shock but persistent undercurrent of risk that supports safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Recent Price Action
factors.summary: Gold down 0.39% in 24h and -0.00% in 4h after three-day rally from $3,941 lows; FXStreet notes failed breakout above 20-day EMA near $4,202 on Friday. Late-stage recovery exhaustion, profit-taking evident.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: US equity futures rising on tech rebound (SK Hynix IPO, AI sector rotation narrative). Risk-on mood neutral for gold; equity strength can compete with gold but no panic or risk-off yet.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & CPI Ahead
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in news window. Market awaits US CPI print (likely this week). Eurozone PPI +5.9% YoY in May (slight beat vs 5.7%); retail sales modest at +0.2% MoM vs +0.3% expected—disinflationary tone.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No mention in news feed of PBoC, RBI, or other large CB buys this cycle. Structural bid absent from view.

factors.summary: No data on GLD/IAU holdings changes in news feed; institutional positioning opaque.

factors.summary: USD/JPY near 162.30 (40-year highs); BoJ and authorities watching closely but no intervention yet. Yen weakness sustained, supporting USD carry trades and dollar strength that pressures gold.

key_levels.support: $4,140 (recent session lows), $4,100, $3,941 (7+ month low from last week)

screening.narrative: Gold has paused its three-day recovery from the $3,941 seven-month low after failing to break above the 20-day EMA (~$4,202) Friday. A firmer US Dollar (DXY retaking 101, USD/JPY near 40-year highs), combined with oil weakness (WTI -0.64% on OPEC+ oversupply fears) and disinflationary signals from Eurozone data, is capping gains. Post-NFP dovish repricing (July Fed hike odds at 24%) provides mild support, but geopolitical tensions (Iran, Ukraine) remain background noise rather than acute catalysts. Price action suggests late-stage profit-taking into a key CPI release expected this week.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI print (likely this week—highest priority for gold direction)
US PCE inflation data (if earlier in week)
Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller)—any hawkish surprise would flip gold bearish
BoJ policy signal or intervention attempt on USD/JPY above 162.30
Continued Strait of Hormuz flow data and OPEC+ production adjustments
screening.entry_guidance: Move has paused after failed breakout; wait for pullback to $4,120–$4,100 (support zone) before reconsidering long. Alternatively, short setup on retest of $4,180–$4,200 resistance with stops above $4,220, targeting $4,100 IF CPI surprise is weak this week.
screening.risk_warning: Surprise hot CPI print (>3.5% core) would trigger sharp gold sell-off and reverse to downtrend; conversely, dovish FOMC guidance or geopolitical escalation (Iran military action, nuclear threat) could spark safe-haven rally above $4,250 quickly, invalidating bearish bias. BoJ verbal or actual intervention could reverse JPY weakness and weaken USD, supporting gold.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS or real yield data in news window. Prior bias history shows sustained NEUTRAL readings; no catalyst to shift real yields materially in next 24-48h absent major inflation or Fed surprise.

factors.summary: USD broadly firm post-Independence Day; EUR/USD at 1.1421 down 0.12% (per FXStreet 15:45), USD/CAD extending advance to ~1.4230. Stronger dollar typically headwind for gold, though magnitude modest.

factors.summary: Fed's Waller speaking from Rome (15:34–15:50 segment) reaffirmed 2% inflation commitment and discussed forward guidance merits; no hawkish surprise or pivot signal. Tone remains data-dependent; no rate decision imminent in next 72h.

factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel (15:24, 15:56) said current price shock 'cannot simply be looked through' and 'not in pre-war situation'—hawkish positioning. ECB may signal readiness for further tightening, supporting EUR relative to commodities and weighing on gold.

factors.summary: ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI eased to 54.0 in June (per 14:00 release), matching estimate but down from 54.5 prior; S&P Global composite 51.9 vs 52.2 preliminary. Activity soft but not recessionary; no inflation shock signal.

factors.summary: TSX index report (16:11) notes gold and oil prices falling; no specific WTI/Brent quotes but narrative is downward pressure post-Iran ceasefire. Lower oil = disinflationary, reduces Fed rate-cut pressure, headwind for gold.

factors.summary: US indices opening with modest gains (Dow +0.02%, S&P +0.24%, Nasdaq +0.53% per 13:40); Trump rang opening bells for 'Trump Accounts' launch. Risk-on tone can compete with safe-haven gold bid, though equity strength historically mixed for XAUUSD.

factors.name: Inflation expectations & breakevens
factors.summary: No fresh inflation data (CPI, PCE) in news window. Silver weakness (50% from Jan peak per MarketWatch 16:08) suggests deflationary concerns, but gold exhibits different technicals; no clear breakeven shift signal.

factors.name: Geopolitical tension
factors.summary: Middle East ceasefire (Iran) appears to be holding; Ukraine and Israel-Lebanon tensions ongoing but not escalating into new war shock. Baseline geopolitical risk contained vs. June levels; limited safe-haven demand impulse.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: Gold -0.36% in 24h, +0.08% in 4h near $4,156.50. Consolidation after prior rebound from 7-month low of $3,941; no directional conviction. Buyer/seller exhaustion not yet evident.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating near $4,156.50 after rebounding from a seven-month low, held in a narrow band by competing cross-currents. USD strength (EUR/USD soft, USD/CAD firm) and ECB hawkish messaging (Schnabel) provide headwinds, while soft ISM Services data and lower oil prices suggest disinflationary pressure that should weigh on Fed rate-cut odds and real yields. However, no fresh catalyst (CPI/PCE, FOMC decision, NFP) arrived in the past 24h to break the stalemate. The market appears to be in a 'wait and see' mode ahead of key data this week.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI (likely mid-week, ~48h out)
ECB rate decision or Lagarde speech (if scheduled)
US NFP and unemployment (early next week if calendar differs)
Fed Chair Powell or other FedSpeak
Oil price action (Brent/WTI trend post-Iran ceasefire)
screening.risk_warning: If US inflation data comes in HOTTER than expected, real yields could spike and gold could face a sharp reversal lower (PHASE 2 bearish). Conversely, if economic data or Fed pivot to dovish signal emerges, gold could break above $4,180.00 on safe-haven/rate-cut anticipation. USD weakness (EUR/USD above 1.1450) would also quickly flip the bias bullish.
screening.entry_guidance: No high-conviction entry warranted in PHASE_1. Hold on sidelines; if gold breaks below $4,120 on CPI miss, consider watching for bounce entries near $4,100. If inflation surprises hot, watch for reversal plays below $4,000 only after sharp momentum exhaustion.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS yield data in news window. Fed Waller comments suggest Fed committed to 2% inflation with policy risks having 'flipped around,' implying cautious stance but no major dovish surprise yet.

factors.summary: Waller (Rome, July 6) emphasizes inflation commitment and forward guidance trade-offs, but stops short of signaling immediate rate cuts. Soft June jobs data trimmed hawkish expectations per FXStreet, but no new FOMC decision or dot plot update in this window.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: FXStreet reports DXY 'trims gains' as fading Fed rate hike bets cap upside (18:12 UTC). USD/JPY +0.58% to 162.30, but broader DXY momentum appears mixed; EUR/USD rebounded sharply off 1.14177 floor, signaling some USD weakness intraday.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI 'runs out of war and out of buyers' per FXStreet (18:08), having given back 3 months of war-driven gains over 3 weeks. Lower oil reduces inflation pressure and removes bid under commodities broadly, including gold.

factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE/PPI data in this window. BoC Q2 survey shows inflation expectations 'declined after Iran ceasefire was signed mid-June,' suggesting disinflationary bias emerging. ECB Wunsch notes 'haven't seen much 2nd round effects' from energy shock, implying near-term inflation risk contained.

factors.summary: Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei funeral procession underway with large crowds (BBC, Al Jazeera 18:50–17:09). New judiciary chief emphasizes 'vengeance' for Khamenei, but market reaction muted; no immediate escalation signal. Peace deal / ceasefire narrative still dominant per ECB Schnabel.

factors.summary: DJIA crossed 53,000 briefly but 'thinks better of it,' Nasdaq tests 200-hour MA repeatedly without sustained breakout. European indices mixed (STOXX 600 -0.35%, DAX +0.15%). Risk-off undertones present but no panic; gold typically benefits on risk-off, but bid is muted.

factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel reiterates current shock 'cannot simply be looked through,' suggesting no rush to ease, but Wunsch tone softer ('not excluding another move, but let's not wait too long'). BoJ yen strength suggests some carry-trade unwinding, USD/JPY still elevated at 162.30; BoE expectations 'steady' per Scotiabank.

factors.summary: FXStreet (16:56) reports gold 'consolidates below $4,200 as traders reassess Fed interest rate outlook.' Price at $4,176.20 shows +0.39% in 4h and +0.11% in 24h—minimal directional conviction. Consolidation after rebound from 7+ month low ($3,941) suggests profit-taking.

factors.name: Central Bank Buying & ETF Flows
factors.summary: No new data on PBoC, RBI, or Turkish CB gold purchases in this news window. No GLD/IAU holdings changes reported. Structural bid historically present but not a current catalyst.

screening.narrative: Gold consolidated below $4,200 on Monday (July 6) after last week's rebound from a 7+ month low, as traders reassess Fed rate-cut expectations in light of softer June jobs data. The USD showed mixed signals—DXY trimmed intraday gains as EUR/USD rallied off the 100-hour MA, while USD/JPY remained elevated. Geopolitical backdrop (Iran funeral, Middle East ceasefire narrative) has calmed, reducing safe-haven urgency. Oil prices continued to fade, removing commodity-bid support. Fed speakers (Waller) maintained a committed-but-cautious stance on inflation with no surprise dovish pivot, and ECB tone shifted slightly softer but not imminent-easing territory. The net result: minimal directional conviction and profit-taking after the recent bounce.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI / Core CPI (likely early-week, July 10–11)
FOMC meeting decision (if scheduled; June FOMC was late June, next likely mid-July)
Nonfarm Payroll (first Friday of month, July 11)
ECB monetary policy communication or data surprises
Middle East escalation risk (Iran retaliation threat if geopolitical sentiment shifts)
Oil price direction (supports or caps inflation narrative)
screening.risk_warning: If USD strengthens materially on firmer Fed expectations (e.g., inflation surprise to upside or Fed officials turn hawkish), gold will likely break below $4,150 support. Conversely, if real yields fall (nominal yields drop sharply or inflation expectations rise) or geopolitical risk spikes (Iran escalates), gold could break above $4,200 and rally toward $4,250+. Profit-taking mode suggests volatility into upcoming data; entry points should await retest of support or resistance after consolidation.
screening.entry_guidance: Price consolidating with minimal 4h/24h momentum. Wait for breakout above $4,200 resistance with USD weakness confirmation, or retest of $4,150 support on potential dip into US CPI release (likely July 10–11). Avoid chasing into this consolidation; risk/reward neutral at current levels.

factors.summary: Bitcoin rebounded after Trump 'big crypto guy' comment (per FXStreet); digital gold narrative may divert some safe-haven flows from physical gold.

screening.narrative: Gold remains trapped in neutral consolidation after a modest post-NFP rebound faded on Monday as resilient US services data and Fed Governor Waller's hawkish tone re-anchored rate expectations. DXY sentiment is at decade-high bullish extremes, real yields remain pressured higher, and equity strength (Dow record, Nasdaq +1.12%) is driving risk-on positioning that competes with gold safe-haven demand. No major economic surprise or Fed pivot catalyst has materialized to break the stalemate. Price action shows micro-volatility (4h +0.07%, 24h +0.10%) with no directional confirmation; three consecutive NEUTRAL 5/10 reads confirm chop.

screening.risk_warning: If Fed pivot accelerates (Powell/other officials signal rate cuts) OR CPI surprises sharply lower in coming weeks, gold will break higher; current NEUTRAL reflects hawkish Fed tone holding. Conversely, if geopolitical escalation (Iran retaliation, NATO tensions spike, China-Taiwan) triggers sharp safe-haven bid, gold could rally past $4,180 despite sticky yields. Equities cracking would also flip gold bullish. Monitor DXY below 100.50 (would ease real-yield pressure) and nominal 10y yield below 4.20% as potential inflection points.
screening.entry_guidance: Price action is choppy and compressed; no directional setup yet. Wait for either a break below $4,150 (short toward $4,100-4,120) or a break above $4,190 (long toward $4,220-4,250) with clear catalyst. If next major CPI/PCE prints cooler-than-expected, re-rate gold bullish; if hotter, expect test of $4,100. Current consolidation typical pre-data; patience advisable.

factors.name: Market Positioning
factors.summary: Hedge funds 'turned most negative on Japanese Yen since 2007' per Bloomberg, suggesting yen carry unwind risk; USD/JPY grinding higher all year. Strong USD bias in positioning headwind for gold.

screening.narrative: Gold remains trapped between two opposing forces: firmer-than-expected US yields and a hawkish Fed tone (bearish) versus nascent disinflation signals (lower oil, Walmart price cuts, supply glut narrative) and soft NFP backdrop. The equity rally on 'lower inflation' hopes suggests real yields may stabilize or tick higher, keeping gold capped. DXY strength and the 'most positive dollar positioning since 2015' remain the dominant headwind. No fresh institutional or geopolitical safe-haven catalyst has emerged today.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (Wednesday Jul 7 or 8 – timing unclear from news; Warsh's first meeting post-guidance strip)
Japan wage/household spending data (Tuesday Jul 7 – lower odds of moving gold directly)
US CPI/PCE release (likely early-mid July, exact date not in news)
ECB rate decision risk (Jul 22-23; Schnabel's hawkish tone on inflation suggests debate persists)
Any further Fed speakers (Waller, Warsh, Powell) – could reinforce hawkish tone
Oil supply shocks or OPEC/Iran developments
Equity drawdown or VIX spike (could trigger safe-haven bid for gold)
screening.risk_warning: If real yields unexpectedly roll over (e.g. on weak upcoming inflation data, risk-off equity spike, or sudden dovish Fed pivot), gold could rally hard through $4,200+. Conversely, if disinflation story accelerates AND Fed stays hawkish (keeping real yields elevated), gold risks breakdown below $4,150. Timing of next major macro data release is critical — currently in blind spot between NFP (last week) and next CPI.
screening.entry_guidance: No clear entry warranted yet; price action at 4h +0.04% shows stalling momentum. If $4,150 support breaks on fresh hawkish catalyst, consider short toward $4,100. If DXY rolls over AND real yields dip, look for break above $4,200 resistance as re-entry opportunity. For now, wait for confirmation either direction — avoid chasing on thin range moves.
